claim_killed: The metallic SL(n) transfer cocycle's full Lyapunov spectrum is symmetric (±-paired) iff n is even — a parity law realizing V29 — and this even-n symmetry is special to the metallic cocycle.
depth_reached: n=2..6, fair broad energy grid vs 3 cherry-picked energies; matched same-companion random control at n=4,6
evidence_quote: On the fair grid the defect **grows monotonically with n** — no even/odd alternation; n=4 (even) is *more* asymmetric than n=3 (odd); n=6 (even) is *not* symmetric. The "law" was a property of three hand-picked energies.
citation_chain: Self-contained kill: reproducer sln_higher_rank.py committed in the arc dir, defect tables (cherry vs fair grid) and the matched-control numbers (0.337 vs 0.344; 0.509 vs 0.503) in-text. B166 is cited only for what SURVIVES (V29 at algebra level), not for the kill.
revival_hypothesis: The EMITTANCE/spectral face B735 — no pre-B735 negative ever consulted the spectral face, and this kill is precisely about a spectrum-symmetry diagnostic; an energy-averaged spectral-face pass could re-pose whether any weaker parity signal survives. Oracle hints (coupling-tier only): dickson_tower (det=-1 parity), symplectic.

claim_killed: The figure-eight character variety's conductor 40=2^3*5 decomposes as (octahedral parent = prime 2) x (golden filling = prime 5), i.e. the prime 2 is the discriminating signature of the Whitehead/Borromean octahedral parent.
depth_reached: 10 two-bridge knots (4_1..9_4), twist and non-twist; bad-prime extraction validated only at genus<=1 (4_1 {2,5}, 5_2 {2,7}), overcounts for genus>=2
evidence_quote: The bad-prime extraction (discriminant of the branch locus) is **reliable only for low genus** — `4_1` ({2,5}, validated) and `5_2` ({2,7}). For genus ≥ 2 (`6_1` up) it **overcounts** (huge spurious primes from the disc-of-disc resultant).
citation_chain: B211: pipeline reproduces B211's 40a1 bad primes {2,5} — yes, validated in-arc. Torn between computed-in-arc (conductor_test.py committed, table in-text) and proxy: the decisive non-twist rows (6_2..9_4) sit in the genus>=2 regime where the branch-locus discriminant is an overcounting stand-in for the true Jacobian conductor, and the arc itself defers that to a specialist — picked the weaker basis (proxy) per the B525 blade.

claim_killed: pi_1 of the silver (m=2) bundle carries a binary octahedral 2O quotient — i.e. its Q(sqrt2) trace-field match with 2O's character field reflects actual group structure (with chat1's supporting claim that all metallic bundles carry 2T and 2I).
depth_reached: all surjections pi_1 -> G enumerated (GAP GQuotients, definitive) for G in {2T(24,3), 2I(120,5), 2O(48,28), S4(24,12)} across golden m004, silver b++RRLL, bronze b++RRRLLL
evidence_quote: **L48 answered, definitively: silver does NOT carry `2O`** (`GQuotients=0`). The `ℚ(√2)` match is a **field-only coincidence**; the group never closes — consistent with `|2O|=48 ≠ p(p²−1)` (B234).
citation_chain: B234: cited as consistency only (|2O| = 48 is never p(p^2-1), so 2O is never SL(2,F_p)) — not load-bearing; the kill itself is the in-arc GAP GQuotients=0 computation (silver_2O.py committed, quotient table in FINDINGS).
revival_hypothesis: Congruence tower B734 — the m004 (4) standard / (8) mod-center split: the mod-center 2-adic structure is exactly where binary/spin-vs-rotation (2O vs S4) distinctions live, so a mod-center tower pass on silver could re-pose whether 2O appears at a central-extension/covering level even though it is never a direct pi_1 quotient. Oracle hints (coupling-tier only): kappa, hyperbolicity_split.
